textPlain: Excited for stuff like this as a CRPG player. A possible future for designing a crpg NPC might be to write a bunch of memories, descriptions, likes and dislikes, etc. in text instead of trying to convey those through branching dialogue.
>call the matrix functions directly in C++Sweet so it can be used in unreal engine it would be awsome to see this used for a local llm game that can generate it's own unique NPCs
Probably I haven’t read the whole article, but it seems to be based on the Russell circumplex model of affect (valence/arousal).https://www.researchgate.net/publication/235361517_A_Circump...
Yeah, that’s the kind of direction I’m hoping for too. It could be hard to follow a narrative without branching dialogue, but maybe it will raise a new kind of NPC
Thanks, that's an interesting idea! Some games need predictable dialogue, but as an option it could be great
